raw_response: I agree with the consensus that the correct answer is C. One additional point to support this view is that patients with β-thalassemia major often have macrocytosis due to bone marrow hyperplasia trying to compensate for chronic anemia, which may also be observed in the peripheral smear along with the expected microcytic hypochromic red blood cells. Therefore, option C remains accurate and comprehensive for describing typical findings in this condition. Answer Choice: C.
